← All worlds/World 01
World 01 · 2 states

Healing

Films for the part of you that is quietly putting itself back together — the slow, unglamorous work of becoming whole again.

Choose an emotion
Heartbreak RecoveryEmotional Numbness
On Emotional Numbness

This is harder to name than sadness. Sadness has a shape, a face, a reason. Numbness is the absence of all of that — the strange flatness where you expected to feel something, the inability to explain what happened to the week. Films are one of the few things that can reach through it. Not by cheering you up, but by being so specific about another person's inner life that, almost by accident, you remember you have one too. These films do not promise to fix the grey. They sit in it patiently, with enough honesty and craft that something, eventually, moves.

Emotional Numbness

Films for when everything is grey and you don’t know why.

25Curated films

25 films — quick browse

Manchester by the Sea

Kenneth Lonergan · 2016

🏆 Won 2 Academy Awards

CS9.4
2016

Manchester by the Sea

Kenneth Lonergan

View Film →

A janitor returns to his hometown after his brother’s death and finds himself the guardian of his teenage nephew — carrying a past he cannot speak.

CS9.3
2021

Drive My Car

Ryusuke Hamaguchi

View Film →

A theatre director rehearsing a multilingual production of Uncle Vanya is paired with a quiet young driver — and slowly begins to speak about his wife.

CS9.5
2022

Aftersun

Charlotte Wells

View Film →

A father and his eleven-year-old daughter spend a summer holiday in Turkey — a memory revisited decades later by the woman she has become.

CS8.6
2017

A Ghost Story

David Lowery

View Film →

After a young musician dies, his ghost — a figure under a sheet — lingers in the house he shared with his wife, watching time pass without him.

CS9.2
2013

Her

Spike Jonze

View Film →

A lonely letter-writer in near-future Los Angeles falls in love with an AI operating system designed to learn him. A slow, devastating arc of being known.

CS8.5
2011

Melancholia

Lars von Trier

View Film →

A bride’s wedding night unravels into depression. Meanwhile, a planet named Melancholia approaches Earth on what may be a collision course.

CS8.7
2008

The Wrestler

Darren Aronofsky

View Film →

A washed-up pro wrestler in his fifties tries to mend his life away from the ring — with a daughter who won’t forgive him and a body that won’t hold up.

CS8.4
2007

Into the Wild

Sean Penn

View Film →

A young man abandons his possessions, savings, and family after graduating, hitchhiking toward Alaska in search of an honest life. Based on a true story.

CS8.8
2020

Nomadland

Chloé Zhao

View Film →

After losing her job and her husband, a woman in her sixties packs her van and becomes a modern nomad, moving from gig to gig through the American West.

CS7.9
2013

The Secret Life of Walter Mitty

Ben Stiller

View Film →

A daydreaming photo archivist at LIFE magazine, faced with losing his job, finally steps outside his fantasies and chases down one missing negative across the world.

CS8.9
1980

Ordinary People

Robert Redford

View Film →

A suburban Chicago family tries to function after a boating accident kills one son and leaves the other with survivor’s guilt and clinical depression.

CS8.6
1999

The Virgin Suicides

Sofia Coppola

View Film →

Narrated by men who were teenagers in 1970s Michigan, remembering the year five sisters died, one by one, and why they could never understand it.

CS8.5
2002

The Hours

Stephen Daldry

View Film →

Three women across three time periods — Virginia Woolf writing Mrs Dalloway, a 1950s housewife reading it, a 2001 New York editor living its themes — braid together.

CS8.3
2005

Broken Flowers

Jim Jarmusch

View Film →

A retired bachelor receives an anonymous letter saying he has a son. He road-trips to visit his five ex-loves to find out which one sent it.

CS8.4
1999

American Beauty

Sam Mendes

View Film →

A suburban man in a dead marriage has a midlife crisis, becomes infatuated with his daughter’s friend, and decides to stop pretending.

CS8.9
2001

The Royal Tenenbaums

Wes Anderson

View Film →

A family of former child prodigies and their estranged father try to reconnect. All of them have been numb since the genius years ended.

CS9.1
2018

Leave No Trace

Debra Granik

View Film →

A war veteran with PTSD and his teenage daughter live off-grid in a Portland forest. When they are discovered, the world tries to absorb them.

CS8.4
2008

Wendy and Lucy

Kelly Reichardt

View Film →

A woman and her dog travel toward Alaska for seasonal work. The dog goes missing in a small Oregon town and the woman cannot afford to leave without her.

CS9.0
2020

Never Rarely Sometimes Always

Eliza Hittman

View Film →

A seventeen-year-old from rural Pennsylvania and her cousin travel to New York City to access reproductive healthcare they cannot get at home.

CS8.7
2001

In the Bedroom

Todd Field

View Film →

A couple in coastal Maine lose their son to violence. The film follows the grief that slowly turns their marriage cold and something else hot.

CS8.2
2017

On Chesil Beach

Dominic Cooke

View Film →

Two young people in 1962 England marry and try to navigate their wedding night. What neither can say destroys everything.

CS8.3
2002

About Schmidt

Alexander Payne

View Film →

A retired actuary from Omaha finds himself without purpose after his wife dies suddenly. He drives his RV to Denver to stop his daughter’s wedding and find meaning.

CS8.0
2003

Elephant

Gus Van Sant

View Film →

An ordinary day at an American high school. The film follows students through their routines before something terrible happens. Based loosely on Columbine.

CS8.8
2010

Beginners

Mike Mills

View Film →

After his mother dies, a seventy-five-year-old man comes out as gay and falls in love for the first time. His son, watching, learns to stop being numb.

CS7.8
2021

Land

Robin Wright

View Film →

A woman devastated by unspecified loss retreats to an isolated Wyoming cabin with no survival skills. A passing stranger keeps her alive.

Films for the long quiet after the door closed.

Next world · 02
Loneliness →

For when no one seems to see you.

Films don't just entertain. They quietly confirm you were seen — long before anyone in your real life knew how to.
